text:
There once was a young inventor named Oliver. He'd been dreaming since childhood about creating gadgets that could change everything — a flying car, perhaps an invisibility cloak. But nothing ever seemed realistic enough.

One rainy night, after pouring endless cups of cocoa, Oliver finally decided enough was enough. Why couldn't he just make something that would save lives? That's when he invented the HeartGuard Monitor. This tiny device wouldn't just keep an eye on blood pressure; it could detect early signs of heart disease.

For weeks Oliver obsessively tinkered. When he finally finished his masterpiece, he went silent. Nobody knew quite what to think. Was he proud? Worried?

Finally, Oliver stepped forward. He presented his creation.

"The HeartGuard won't save everyone," he began, looking sober. "But think of how many lives could be saved. Children could play soccer. Seniors could enjoy their gardens."

A silence fell.

"I know," Oliver said softly. "That's why I want
